codeReview.summary: The feature adds testnet wiring and a performance report, but the new testnet indexer fallback only polls transactions from the contract account. On Aptos this endpoint covers account-sent transactions, so user-submitted trades/orders are not reliably indexed, which can break live frontend correctness goals for testnet.

issues.description: Testnet/devnet mode is forced into REST fallback (line 138) and polls `GET /accounts/{contractAddress}/transactions` (line 268). This endpoint only returns transactions for that account, so events emitted from other users' transactions are missed. Result: orderbook/trades/balances can become stale for normal frontend user activity, conflicting with integration expectations (e.g., live trade/candle updates after user swaps).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill does not document the Aptos testnet events-v1 deprecation path or safe alternatives/limitations when using REST fallback.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md has no guidance on indexer deprecation or fallback strategy; handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges explicitly requests adding this.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared library note on events-v1 deprecation lacks a critical caveat that account-transaction polling by contract address does not capture all user-generated module events.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/aptos-events-v1-deprecation.md notes deprecation but not the account-tx scope limitation; implementation uses `/accounts/${contract}/transactions` in api/src/indexer/event-indexer.ts:268.

summary: Fail. The main blocker is the testnet indexer fallback scope: polling only contract-account transactions can miss user-driven events, so frontend live testnet data integrity is not guaranteed.
